mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate synchronized demand-supply contraction, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with Rank IC > 0.05 when tested against single-series baselines over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, rising real activity measured by GDP growth accelerates inflation expectations, prompting aggressive policy tightening that suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually and CPI rises above 3.0% during periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in retail and food services sales reflects expanding consumer spending power, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistically significant difference between positive and negative RSAFS growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 115.0, supply chain capacity constraints emerge, driving input cost inflation that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower when INDPRO crosses above 115.0 compared to periods below that threshold (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising employment and elevated federal funds rates signals an overheating economy where wage-push inflation forces sustained monetary restraint, ultimately depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-FEDFUNDS signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline in out-of-sample Rank IC testing over a 5-year window, or forward returns are not significantly negative when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market downturns, declining housing starts amplify the negative impact of rising unemployment on consumer confidence, triggering a sharp equity sell-off and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline significantly when HOUST falls below 1600.0 and UNRATE rises, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.
